lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Small Talk
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tânia Rosa | Aula 11 -- Small Talk

listenings.text: Hi there. Wonderful museum, isn't it? I noticed you're looking at the same painting as me. Are you visiting the city too? I'm from Australia, and this is my first trip to Europe. The art here is incredible, isn't it? I love how welcoming everyone has been. Funny thing, I met a couple from Brazil on the train this morning. Small world, right? Well, I should let you enjoy the rest of the gallery. It was lovely chatting with you. Have a wonderful day.

For years, I traveled the world but kept to myself. I would smile at people and then look away, afraid of saying the wrong thing. But one day, a kind stranger in a cafe started a simple conversation with me, and everything changed. I realized that a friendly comment about the food or the view is all you need to begin. Now, wherever I go, I am the one who breaks the ice. I have made friends on planes, in markets, and in long lines. You don't need perfect grammar to connect with someone. You just need the courage to smile and say hello.

extra_audio.text: First, a stranger smiles at Tania and comments on the wonderful museum. Then he asks if she is visiting the city as well. Next, Tania says she is from Brazil and they discover a coincidence. After that, they chat about the welcoming people and the beautiful art. Finally, they wish each other a lovely day and say goodbye.
